title: PLA 3 Sorties Around Taiwan April 5 — All Cross Median Line; YTD Incursions Down 46.5%
detail: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense reported 3 PLA aircraft sorties operating around Taiwan on April 5, 2026 — a slight uptick from 2 sorties on April 3 but well below the late-March peak of 19 sorties (March 29). All 3 sorties crossed the median line. GlobalSecurity.org reported Taiwan MND data showing the year-to-date (January–April 2026) PLA aircraft deployed into Taiwan's ADIZ represent a 46.5% decrease compared to the same period in 2025, with 460 sorties YTD versus a comparable 2025 figure. Analysts note the YTD decrease reflects both the National People's Congress two-week lull (early March) and the diplomatic context of the KMT-China engagement and the Trump-Xi summit preparation. PLA naval vessels continued operating around Taiwan. With 2 days before KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's April 7 China departure, the 3-sortie count is consistent with PLA maintaining sustained low-level presence rather than signaling through elevated air activity — allowing diplomatic channels to take the lead in cross-strait signaling ahead of the KMT chair's Xi meeting.

title: Analysis: KMT Chair Cheng's China Trip 'Fraught with Risk' — Internal Party Unease Deepens Two Days Before Departure
detail: With KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's April 7 China departure two days away, Asia Times published an analysis titled 'Taiwan opposition leader's China trip is fraught with risk,' documenting growing internal KMT concerns about the strategic and political risks of the visit. Pakistan Today and other regional outlets reported on April 5 that Taiwan opposition leader Cheng was visiting China ahead of the Trump-Xi meeting — framing the trip explicitly as a Beijing diplomatic effort to shape summit dynamics. The analysis identified three key risk vectors: (1) Domestic political exposure — KMT members are divided between those viewing the trip as a peace initiative and those fearing it will be exploited by Beijing; (2) Defense budget optics — the visit occurs while KMT is blocking Taiwan's NT$1.25T special defense budget, creating a narrative that KMT is pursuing Beijing's diplomatic agenda at the expense of Taiwan's security; and (3) Summit interference — Beijing's use of the KMT visit to project cross-strait 'goodwill' to President Trump ahead of the May 14-15 summit may create diplomatic pressure on the US-Taiwan relationship. Cheng's stated objectives are to 'extend kindness, build mutual trust, and usher in a new spring in cross-strait relations' — language that Beijing has received as consistent with its own unification narrative.

media.caption: Analysis: KMT Chair Cheng's China Trip 'Fraught with Risk' — Internal Party Unease Deepens Two Days Before Departure
